item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always questioned the aptness of the "shepherd and his flock" metaphor for religion.
A shepherd only takes care of the flock in order to exploit them. Even the most gentle keeper does not hesitate to slaughter his charges whenever it most benefits himself.

Doesn't seem like the right tone for a loving sky-Daddy.

So I'm amused by this. In particular, I'm imagining it as some shallow (probably slightly stoned) an-artist's critique of modern religion. A physical manifestation of "wake up sheep-ple!"

But it really needs a once over for copy-editing. There are a lot of preposition and article errors typical of non-native English speakers. More than I can list here, or would be comfortable editing without permission because they'd add up to a fairly major revision.

Which leaves me at a neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Long time waiting, but the plural is "Oxen"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm gonna hold my vote for now, there are a couple nitpicks I want to address first.

-Why would a God of Lambs need to be talked to in a human language?

-The issue of SCP staff going "Weird thing? IT'S ALIENS OH EM GEE" has already been addressed.

-And finally: guess it might just be me, but it kinda itches me that a foreign object –alien or otherwise, but supposedly non-human in the making– would so sistematically stick to a strictly human label, namely by only affecting critters of the genus Ovis. Taxonomy is a purely human artifact in order to better inter-human communication, so why would an alien wanna stick to it in favor of a more general, say, "woolly thingies that eat veggies" kinda label? For instance, is the Foundation sure this thing wouldn't work with alpacas, llamas or musk ox herds1, or anyway any woolly, human-enslaved beast that is not immediately reachable in Germany?

On a second thought, points 1 and 3 would be explained if
+ show block
– hide block

the thing here was human made (what for, though?), which would also explain the sheep going "THE POLYHEDRON IS A LIE" at the end. Still…

Footnotes
1. What in the name of everything baa is the plural of ox? Oxes? Oxs? Oxx? English is weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anybody for a little Goat Simulator? I was definitely having flashbacks to the Goat God portion…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole "oh they can talk now" kinda jarred me a bit but it made up for it with how silly the entire SCP as a whole was. Good read.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha. Enjoyable read. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it

item.title: Staff Post (Site/Forum Crit Team. Open)
item.content:encoded:
This is objectively bad advice. Random "gratuitous" (your use of this word made me think you were being sarcastic at first! This word literally means "uncalled for; lacking good reason; unwarranted.") [DATA EXPUNGED] is perhaps the most common newbie mistake. Just because other articles in the past have expunged a lot of information does not mean that this is either called for or desirable. A little targeted expungement can add an air of mystery, but it's a difficult trick to pull off well. Most modern articles don't expunge anything.

E: To clarify, expunging in this way generally gives the impression that the author is lazy, obtuse, or otherwise couldn't be arsed to come up with a sufficiently compelling story, and is relying on the audience's interpretation to make their work interesting.

I recommend the guide Zen and the art of DATA EXPUNGED for more information on how to use this device correctly, and common ways it goes wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why we ask that newbies refrain from giving advice until they have more experience under their belt. More [DATA EXPUNGED] almost never improves an article. Usually it just makes it less interesting. I highly recommend that you try to get more a of a feel for the site and do more reading before you start handing out advice like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this post could benefit from some gratuitous [DATA EXPUNGED]. IMO you go into to much detail about what the sheep do (crushing the skull, trampling, etc) and how they do it, when you could instead imply that whatever they are doing is too terrifying to mention.
I also agree with DisquietRiot in that the talking sheep was a little too much. Keep it simpler, or use some [REDACTED] [DATA EXPUNGED]. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sheep for the sheep god!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed a bit the description. It should be less vague now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture comes from here, and is used with permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was immediately reminded of the episode of South Park where the cows start worshipping the cow statue/clock thing. When the sheep started talking, I had to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1843 and instances of SCP-1843-1 during testing in a Foundation’s property.

This sounds odd. Maybe have it be "during testing in Foundation property"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Went in a pretty underwhelming direction imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I wanted them to be terminated. Would it be better that way?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please upload the picture directly to this page. I hate looking at broken images.

Edit: Came back to read this today, and I'm not particularly thrilled. There are some problems with the description of the object (you specifically say 'silicon' but then finish up with 'metal and plastic', which is really really vague). Also, for going in a religious direction with this, it's a bit… simple, isn't it? I'd expect more iconography, for the ritual to be more elaborate. Got some serious missed opportunities here.

0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something just kinda felt…off with this one. It seems like you took any old cultist artifact, and swapped in sheep for humans. It's not badly written, but it felt bland in a certain way. No vote.

Also, how did the Foundation just assume that it could be extraterrestrial? It seems like kind of a big jump to be taking, logically speaking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article has some tonal issues and it uses tranquilizers in a manner that isn't really realistic, but I like it anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can dig the idea, because I also enjoy the meerkat one and think this has enough differences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1843-1-26 appeared to emit a vocalization similar to "lugner", the German word for "liar.

lügner. with ü, not u.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to second Aelanna on the similarity to SCP-1143. The SCP designation of the affected sheep seems unnecessary. We usually don't classify the victims of SCPs. Also, I don't buy the sheep praying for deliverance from us. The sheep actually have a pretty good deal, in general.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of the meerkat monolith, except not as charming to me. It also suffers from improper sub-object notation: if you're going to have sub-objects, then the object should be -1 and the affected ovine organisms as -2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I was saying, "Oh, more religious animals. Whoop-dee-do." Then I saw the rest. You have secured my upvote, even if this is already done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's enjoy some gratuitous sheep violence, shall we?
